title: NIH Office of the Director | National Institutes of Health (NIH)
content: List of Institutes and Centers NIH Office of the Director Office of the Principal Deputy Director Administration and Services Office of Communications and Public Liaison Office of Legislative Policy and Analysis (OLPA) Office of Management Assessment (OMA) Executive Secretariat (ExecSec) Science Policy Research, Funding and Coordination Directors of NIH Institutes and Centers NIH Office of the Director The Office of the Director is the central office at NIH, and is responsible for setting policy for NIH and for planning, managing, and coordinating the programs and activities of all the NIH components. The NIH Director plays an active role in shaping the agency's activities and outlook. With a unique and critical perspective on the whole of the NIH, the Director is responsible for providing leadership to the Institutes and Centers , and for constantly identifying needs and opportunities, especially for efforts that involve multiple Institutes. The NIH Director NIH Leadership NIH Organization This page last reviewed on September 2, 2025
